title: Machilus minutiflorus
intro: Machilus minutiflorus, also known as the small-flowered machilus, is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae. The small-flowered machilus is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of coughs, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Machilus minutiflorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Machilus minutiflorus is commonly used to make tea, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Machilus minutiflorus is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae. The Lauraceae family is a group of flowering plants that also includes laurel, bay laurel, and avocados. The Machilus genus is part of the tribe Phoebeae and is native to Asia. This plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree, and its classification falls within the phylum Angiospermophyta. Further classification of Machilus minutiflorus includes its kingdom as Plantae.
